meta.title: BEM Methodology – Scalable CSS Architecture (2026)
meta.description: Master BEM (Block-Element-Modifier) methodology for maintainable, scalable CSS. Learn naming conventions, file structure, and best practices for team collaboration.

content.value: BEM (Block-Element-Modifier) is a popular CSS naming methodology that helps create scalable, maintainable, and reusable components. It provides a clear structure for class names, making it easier for teams to collaborate and understand the relationship between HTML and CSS.

Featured Article

This is an amazing article about BEM methodology.
Title

value: **Avoid deep nesting** – Keep elements flat (`.block__element` not `.block__element__subelement`)
**Use modifiers for variations** – Not separate blocks
**Combine with utility classes** – For spacing, typography utilities
**Be consistent** – Same naming rules across entire project
**Document your components** – Create a style guide/pattern library
**Use BEM with CSS-in-JS** – Maintains naming consistency
**Never use IDs for styling** – IDs break reusability and specificity
**Modifiers without base block** – Missing `.button` class when using `.button--primary`
**Using elements outside blocks** – HTML structure must match BEM naming

BEM methodology provides a clear, scalable approach to CSS architecture. By using consistent naming patterns (Block__Element--Modifier), teams can build maintainable components that are reusable, self-documenting, and resistant to style conflicts. While class names become more verbose, the benefits of predictability and encapsulation far outweigh the costs for large-scale projects.

faq.question: What does BEM stand for?
faq.answer: Block, Element, Modifier – a CSS naming methodology for creating reusable components.

faq.question: Why use double underscores and hyphens?
faq.answer: Convention: __ for elements, -- for modifiers. This creates visual distinction and makes relationships clear.

faq.question: Can I nest BEM elements?
faq.answer: Avoid deep nesting (like .block__element__subelement). Keep flat: .block__element and .block__subelement.

faq.question: Does BEM work with CSS-in-JS?
faq.answer: Yes! BEM principles (encapsulation, naming patterns) apply well to CSS-in-JS solutions like styled-components.

faq.question: Should I use BEM with utility frameworks like Tailwind?
faq.answer: They can coexist. Use BEM for component structure and Tailwind utilities for spacing, colors, etc. Or choose one primary approach.
